string.text:
The CZ 805 BREN is a gas-operated assault rifle designed and manufactured in Czechoslovakia by Ceska zbrojovka Uhersky Brod, developed to replace the Vz. 58 in the Armed Forces of the Czech Republic in 2006. It and its variants has been exported to be adopted by other countries like France, Mexico, and Ukraine.
 This model is chambered for 5.56?45mm NATO rounds.
 %c[ui_gray_2]ATTACHMENTS: 
 %c[d_purple] - %c[ui_gray_2] Scopes: Most scopes for Picatinny/weaver
 %c[d_purple] - %c[ui_gray_2] Suppressor: Surefire SOCOM556-MONSTER

The CZ 805 BREN is a gas-operated assault rifle designed and manufactured in Czechoslovakia by Ceska zbrojovka Uhersky Brod, developed to replace the Vz. 58 in the Armed Forces of the Czech Republic in 2006. It and its variants has been exported to be adopted by other countries like France, Mexico, and Ukraine.
 This model is chambered for 6.8 Remington SPC rounds.
 %c[ui_gray_2]ATTACHMENTS:
 %c[d_purple] - %c[ui_gray_2] Scopes: Most scopes for Picatinny/weaver
 %c[d_purple] - %c[ui_gray_2] Suppressor: Surefire SOCOM556-MONSTER

The CZ 805 BREN is a gas-operated assault rifle designed and manufactured in Czechoslovakia by Ceska zbrojovka Uhersky Brod, developed to replace the Vz. 58 in the Armed Forces of the Czech Republic in 2006. It and its variants has been exported to be adopted by other countries like France, Mexico, and Ukraine.
 This model is chambered for 5.56?45mm NATO rounds.
 This particular weapon has a front grip attached, and has been painted with a red skull camouflage.
 
 %c[ui_gray_2]ATTACHMENTS: 
 %c[d_purple] - %c[ui_gray_2] Scopes: Most scopes for Picatinny/weaver.
 %c[d_purple] - %c[ui_gray_2] Suppressor: Surefire SOCOM556-MONSTER
